title: Dynamic Import & Code Splitting
description:
Implement code splitting using `React.lazy` and `Suspense` for a multi-route dashboard where each panel is loaded on demand.

### Requirements

- Each dashboard panel (`Analytics`, `Settings`, `Reports`) is loaded via `React.lazy`
- Wrap lazy components in `Suspense` with a custom `LoadingFallback`
- The `LoadingFallback` shows a skeleton matching the panel layout
- Handle load failures with an `ErrorBoundary` that shows a retry button
- Clicking retry re-attempts the lazy import
- Preload the next likely panel on hover over its tab button
- Track which panels have been loaded (show a "loaded" badge)

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Tab button | `panel-tab-{id}` |
| Panel content | `panel-content` |
| Loading fallback | `loading-fallback` |
| Error boundary | `error-boundary` |
| Retry button | `retry-btn` |
| Loaded badge | `loaded-{id}` |
constraints:
- Must use React.lazy and Suspense
- Must implement a class-based ErrorBoundary
- Retry must actually re-attempt the import
- Preload on hover using dynamic import()
- Panels must only load when first accessed
hints: React.lazy(() => import('./Panel')) returns a lazy component. Store the import function in a map so you can retry it later.
For preload on hover, call the import() function but don't use its result — React's cache will remember it.
An ErrorBoundary is a class component with static getDerivedStateFromError and componentDidCatch.
